name: NTFP-TEJPATA-BN-25KG
description: Sun-dried Cinnamomum tamala leaves from the forests of West Bengal's Jalpaiguri and Alipurduar districts. Strong aromatic profile, hand-sorted, free from stems. Used widely in culinary spice blends, curry mixes, and Ayurvedic formulations.

name: NTFP-SALSEED-JH-50KG
description: Premium quality Sal seeds (Shorea robusta) collected by Jharkhand forest-dwelling communities under FRA. Rich in fat content (44–50%), used in confectionery butter, edible fat, and cosmetics. Machine-cleaned and graded.

name: NTFP-MAHUAFLWR-OD-25KG
description: Shade-dried Madhuca longifolia flowers from Odisha's Sundargarh and Keonjhar districts. High sugar content (65–70%), used in distillation, animal feed manufacturing, confectionery, and traditional fermented beverage production. FRA-compliant community harvest.

name: NTFP-WILDHONEY-NE-500ML
description: Unprocessed wild honey sourced from natural rock hive and tree hive collections across Meghalaya, Assam, and Arunachal Pradesh. Multi-floral, dark amber, high pollen count. Not heat-treated. FSSAI compliant packaging. Min moisture < 20%.

name: NTFP-AMLA-DR-OD-25KG
description: Naturally dried Phyllanthus emblica (Amla) from Odisha community forest lands. Deseeded and sliced before sun-drying. Very high Vitamin C (600–700mg/100g). Used in nutraceuticals, Ayurvedic churna, hair-care formulations, and food supplements.

name: NTFP-BAMBOSHT-NE-10KG
description: Fermented and sun-dried bamboo shoots (meizithei/soibum) from Manipur and Nagaland. Traditional processing by indigenous communities. Used in regional cuisine, Asian food export, and flavour concentrates. Strong fermented aroma typical of authentic product.

name: NTFP-KARANJSEED-WB-50KG
description: Pongamia pinnata seeds collected from West Bengal and Jharkhand farmland borders and community forests. 27–39% oil content. Used in biofuel production, soap manufacture, and as organic pest repellent. Bulk wholesale, unsorted pods acceptable.

name: NTFP-WILDTURMERIC-NE-25KG
description: Curcuma aromatica (wild turmeric / kasturi haldi) rhizomes from Assam and Tripura. Higher curcumin content than cultivated varieties (4–6%). Used in Ayurvedic skincare, pharmaceutical extracts, and natural dye production. Shade-dried, unwashed.

name: NTFP-KENDU-OD-BNDL
description: Diospyros melanoxylon (Kendu/Tendu) leaves from Odisha and Jharkhand forests. State-regulated collection under Odisha Forest Produce (Control of Trade) Act. Used in traditional bidi manufacturing. Sold in standardised bundles of 50 leaves.

name: NTFP-MAHUASEED-OD-50KG
description: Madhuca longifolia seeds (locally called Ilupa in Odisha) with 35–40% oil content. Used in edible oil production, soap making, leather processing, and pharmaceutical base oils. Collected during May–June harvest season by tribal communities.

name: NTFP-MUSHROOM-NE-5KG
description: Mixed wild mushrooms (Cantharellus, Boletus, and Pleurotus species) sun-dried from Nagaland and Manipur forest floors. Harvested July–September. High umami flavour, used in gourmet cooking, ramen bases, and nutraceutical powders. Food-grade pack.

name: NTFP-TAMARIND-JH-25KG
description: Wild Tamarindus indica pods from Jharkhand and Bihar Sal forest zones. Darker, more acidic profile compared to cultivated varieties. Deseeded and compressed blocks. Used in condiments, chutney manufacturing, and tamarind paste production.
